prompt: The method reference does not compile. What is the bug?
choices: Method references cannot reference methods with parameters

triple is an instance method; referencing it as ClassName::method without an instance produces a Function<MethodRefBug, Integer> not Function<Integer, Integer>; use an instance reference: new MethodRefBug()::triple
Method references to non-static methods must use super:: syntax
multiplier must be static for method references to work
explanation: An unbound instance method reference ClassName::instanceMethod creates a function that takes the receiver as the first argument. MethodRefBug::triple fits BiFunction<MethodRefBug, Integer, Integer> or requires an instance reference. Fix: MethodRefBug obj = new MethodRefBug(); Function<Integer,Integer> fn = obj::triple;
